content.problemStatement:
MoEngage has given you an array DDD of length NNN. Construct a graph with NNN vertices such that:


The graph is connected and does not contain any parallel edges or self-loops;
Let dud_udu​ be the degree of vertex uuu (number of edges incident to uuu). Then, du≤Dud_u \le D_udu​≤Du​;
⊕i=1Ndi=0\oplus_{i=1}^{N} d_i = 0⊕i=1N​di​=0. In other words, the bitwise XOR of the degrees of all the vertices is 000.
If multiple such graphs exist, print any.


Input Format

The first line of input contains one integer TTT - the number of test cases you have to process. The descriptions of TTT test cases follow.
The first line contains one integer NNN - the size of the array DDD and the number of vertices to be present in the graph.
The second line contains NNN integers D1,D2,…,DND_1, D_2, \ldots, D_ND1​,D2​,…,DN​ - the elements of the array DDD.


Output Format
For each test case:



If it is impossible to construct such a graph, print NO\texttt{NO}NO in a single line.
Otherwise, print YES\texttt{YES}YES in a single line. Then, in the next NNN lines, print the adjacency matrix of the graph.

The ithi^{th}ith line should contain a string AiA_iAi​ of length NNN. The jthj^{th}jth character of AiA_iAi​ is equal to `1` if there is an edge between the vertices iii and jjj and `0` if there is no edge.

**Note:** The strings YES\texttt{YES}YES and NO\texttt{NO}NO are **case-sensitive**.
constraints: Sum of N2N^2N2 over all test cases does not exceed 10710^7107.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: A possible graph satisfying all the conditions is:

Here, the degrees of the vertices is d=[1,1,2,2]d = [1, 1, 2, 2]d=[1,1,2,2]. Note that, di≤Did_i \leq D_idi​≤Di​ (1≤i≤4)(1 \leq i \leq 4)(1≤i≤4). Also, the bitwise XOR of the degrees of all vertices is 1⊕1⊕2⊕2=01 \oplus 1 \oplus 2 \oplus 2 = 01⊕1⊕2⊕2=0.
Test case 222: There exists no graph that satisfies all the given conditions.
Test case 333: There exists no graph that satisfies all the given conditions.
Test case 444: A possible graph satisfying all the conditions is:

Here, the degrees of the vertices is d=[2,3,2,1,2]d = [2, 3, 2, 1, 2]d=[2,3,2,1,2]. Note that, di≤Did_i \leq D_idi​≤Di​ (1≤i≤5)(1 \leq i \leq 5)(1≤i≤5). Also, the bitwise XOR of the degrees of all vertices is 2⊕3⊕2⊕1⊕2=02 \oplus 3 \oplus 2 \oplus 1 \oplus 2 = 02⊕3⊕2⊕1⊕2=0.
